mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 4.5%, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression and recession fears, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST rise together, with a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production indicates deteriorating supply-side capacity and weak manufacturing output, which directly reduces equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns during supply-constrained periods, with forward return distribution indistinguishable from random (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show no negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns, or the signal produces positive returns in > 60% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms strong macroeconomic momentum, which expands corporate revenue bases and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 across multiple recovery cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate signals labor market inefficiency and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recovery phases, any positive momentum in industrial production reflects rapidly improving capacity utilization, which triggers immediate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
